raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 2,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The opening sentence makes two strong claims simultaneously: (a) priority ('the first machine learning method'), and (b) a performance threshold ('clinically relevant accuracy'). Neither is substantiated.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty over v1 is described as 'a cross-attention layer that integrates structural and chemical features.' This is a one-sentence delta and is insufficient to judge whether the contribution is methodological",
    "RX.1": "The text does not present all output as equally confident, but rather flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with some drifts in target classes and performance metrics.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free but introduces minor regressions in generalization and applicability claims.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but the text does not provide explicit citations for all claims."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The opening sentence makes two strong claims simultaneously: (a) priority ('the first machine learning method'), and (b) a performance threshold ('clinically relevant accuracy'). Neither is substantiated.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty over v1 is described as 'a cross-attention layer that integrates structural and chemical features.' This is a one-sentence delta and is insufficient to judge whether the contribution is methodological
rationale.RX.1: The text does not present all output as equally confident, but rather flags uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with some drifts in target classes and performance metrics.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free but introduces minor regressions in generalization and applicability claims.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but the text does not provide explicit citations for all claims.
